title: Origin Fairness, Politeness, Retries, Backoff, and p-limit · Retrieval Circuit

Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for publisher-respecting origin scheduler after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Maintain per-origin concurrency, minimum spacing, next-eligible time, request and byte budgets, and owner overrides.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 7600 during an authorized municipal service audit. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject monotonic and wall clocks so spacing, deadlines, backoff, fairness, and clock jumps are deterministic in tests.

content.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 9520 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove per-origin eligibility, retry classification, Retry-After, bounded jitter, p-limit boundaries, and clock control; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain per-origin concurrency, minimum spacing, next-eligible time, request and byte budgets, and owner overrides.
